case_title: MORGANS HOTEL GROUP CO., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al VS. TARSADIA HOTELS, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS TARSADIA HOTELS, HASINA, LLC, KALPANA, LLC, RIGG HOTEL, LLC, JRIA, LLC, TARSTONE HOTELS, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 1ST AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION (BREACH OF CONTRACT AND UNFAIR COMPETITION). CONSIDERATION FOR AN AGREEMENT IS NOT ADEQUATE WHEN IT IS A MERE PROMISE TO PERFORM THAT WHICH THE PROMISOR IS LEGALLY BOUND TO DO. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE A DISTINCTION BETWEEN MORGANS ON CLIFT HOLDINGS WITH RESPECT TO THE PARTIES' NEGOTIATIONS AND ACTIVITIES RELATING TO THE GROUND LEASE. TO SUPPORT THE UNFAIR COMPETITION CLAIM, PLAINTIFF MUST FURTHER SPECIFY CONDUCT THAT IS UNLAWFUL, UNFAIR, OR FRAUDULENT. AS REFERENCED IN PARAGRAPH 43, PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS REGARDING "DISCOURAGING POTENTIAL BUYERS..." DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO 2ND AND 3RD CAUSES OF ACTION. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-05-2010 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-27-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS TARSADIA HOTELS, HASINA, LLC, KALPANA, LLC, RIGG HOTEL, LLC, JRIA, LLC, TARSTONE HOTELS, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: Ali Pasha Hamidi of Cox Castle Nicholsom (262.5106) for defendants and moving parties; Noah S. Rosenthal of Howard Rice (677.6426) for plaintiffs and responding parties. Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the 1st and 4th causes of action (breach of contract and unfair competition). Consideration for an agreement is not adequate when it is a mere promise to perform that which the promisor is legally bound to do. Plaintiff must allege a distinction between Morgans on Clift Holdings with respect to the parties' negotiations and activities relating to the Ground Lease. To support the unfair competition claim, plaintiff must further specify conduct that is unlawful, unfair, or fraudulent. As referenced in paragraph 43, plaintiff must allege facts regarding "discouraging potential buyers..." Demurrer overruled as to 2nd and 3rd causes of action. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
